book_claims.claim: Fresh and dried cherries are suggested as dessert options in the Longevity Diet, with 100 g fresh or 20 g dried as a serving.
book_claims.mechanism: Whole-fruit dessert replacement reduces refined-sugar intake while delivering antioxidant polyphenols; fits the low-calorie, nutrient-dense dessert pattern associated with longevity-promoting diets.

supplementary_research.finding: Daily consumption of tart cherry juice (480 mL) reduced systolic blood pressure and LDL cholesterol in older adults, with improvements attributed to anthocyanins and polyphenolic antioxidants — cardiovascular benefits directly relevant to longevity.

supplementary_research.source: Howatson et al., Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports (2010) — PMID 19957941
supplementary_research.finding: Tart cherry juice supplementation significantly reduced markers of exercise-induced inflammation and oxidative stress (IL-6, CRP, TBARS), consistent with the anti-inflammatory longevity mechanisms promoted by the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.source: Chai et al., Journal of Nutritional Biochemistry (2019) — PMID 31136926
supplementary_research.finding: Anthocyanins from cherries activate AMPK and Nrf2 pathways in adipocytes, reducing fat accumulation and oxidative stress — mechanisms associated with metabolic health and extended healthspan.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Rapidly absorbed in small intestine; peak plasma levels at 1–2 hours; bioavailability increased with whole-fruit consumption versus isolated extracts.
Bioavailable oral melatonin; supports circadian rhythm regulation and sleep quality, a key pillar of the Longevity Diet's lifestyle recommendations.
Water-soluble; absorbed via sodium-dependent transporters; contributes to collagen synthesis and antioxidant defense.

synergies.description: Cherry anthocyanins and cocoa flavanols share complementary anti-inflammatory mechanisms, acting on overlapping NF-kB and COX-2 pathways.
Dietary fat from almonds enhances absorption of fat-soluble cherry polyphenols; classic Mediterranean dessert pairing.
